nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=115 beats='Saturday morning at the Maple County Storm Chasers Club, a junior weather club that meets in the grange hall on Quarry Road; you bike up under a sky the color of dishwater while the radio in the window mutters to itself; today is the day the big autumn storm crosses the county, and every member from eight to eighty has a job; you rack your bike and hurry for the door'>>

choices.label: Head inside for the morning briefing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='inside, the hall hums; Ms. Okafor, the club leader and a retired weather-service forecaster, taps the wall map: a squall line, a long marching wall of storms, will cross Maple County this afternoon; she reads the rules the club lives by, nobody goes anywhere without an adult, every post shelters when its adult calls it, and weather is to be respected, never feared; then she asks for volunteers for the three teams'>>

choices.label: Step up when she calls for volunteers.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=125 beats='Ms. Okafor says the club needs you on exactly one team today, your pick, and the teams will not cross paths again until the storm is done; RADIO runs the attic comms post with Marisol, the club's licensed operator, relaying storm-spotter reports; MAPS runs the ops table down here with her and Dev, plotting the storm's track; FIELD KIT rides in the club van with Ray and Priya, two trained adult spotters, to run instruments from the storm's safe side; three doors, one storm'>>

choices.label: Join the RADIO team in the attic comms post.
Join the MAPS team at the ops table.
Join the FIELD KIT team in the club van.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the attic stairs creak up into the comms post: two radios glowing, a county wall map bristling with pins, headsets on hooks; Marisol, the club's licensed operator, pats the second chair and says that today you are her second seat; through the round window the western sky is stacking up dark'>>

choices.label: Take the second seat and put on a headset.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Marisol teaches fast: say the club callsign, speak slow, write everything in the log with the time; the first spotter check-ins crackle through, Cedar Flats, Pine Hollow, Route 9, and you log each one; outside, the wind is starting to lean on the grange hall'>>

choices.label: Keep logging as the net wakes up.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='two problems at once: an ugly crackle is chewing the main antenna signal, and check-ins are stacking up faster than one person can log; Marisol says she can only be in one place at a time, so the two of you will have to split the work'>>

choices.label: Help trace the crackling antenna cable.
Take the logbook and run check-ins while Marisol traces it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='flashlight in your teeth, you follow the feedline from the radio along the rafters while Marisol taps each joint; dust, cobwebs, the wind singing through the vents; the crackle in the monitor speaker gets louder every time the line moves'>>

choices.label: Follow the line to where it moves the most.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='two suspects: at the round window the connector is green with corrosion, and near the spool of spare cable a section has been squashed flat under a shifted beam; Marisol checks her watch and says there is time to fix one of them properly before the squall line arrives'>>

choices.label: Weigh the two fixes with Marisol.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='Marisol lays out the choice like a real technician: reseat and seal the corroded window connector, a small fix in a tight spot, or bypass the squashed section by running the spare cable up over the rafters, a bigger fix with fresh line'>>

choices.label: Clean and reseat the corroded window connector.
Run the spare cable over the rafters to bypass the damage.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='you sand the green crust until the copper shines, seat the connector with a click, and wrap it in rubber tape the way Marisol shows you; the crackle drops away like a hand lifted off the speaker'>>

choices.label: Head back to the radio desk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the signal meter climbs and holds; spotter voices come through clean; Marisol looks at the clock, the squall line is maybe twenty minutes out, and reminds you that a smart operator proves a repair before trusting it, but the net is getting busy'>>

choices.label: Decide how much you trust your fix.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='test it or trust it: you could run a radio check with Pine Hollow, the farthest spotter on the net, which costs a quiet minute you may want later, or jump straight back into the net traffic and let the climbing meter be your proof'>>

choices.label: Run a radio check with far-off Pine Hollow.
Trust the meter and dive back into the net.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='you key up: Pine Hollow, this is the grange hall attic, radio check; a heartbeat of hiss, then the reply booms back louder than it has sounded all season; Marisol writes your fix and the test time into the maintenance log'>>

choices.label: Rejoin the net with a proven antenna.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='the squall line arrives with a drumroll of rain on the roof; check-ins pour in, hail at Dutchman's Corner, gusts on Route 9, and every voice rides your repaired line clean into the log and onward to the weather service'>>

choices.label: Ride the storm at the radio until it passes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=92 beats='the line sweeps east and the sun cuts under the clouds; the weather service calls the club's relay the clearest in the county; Marisol shakes your hand, operator style, and writes your name beside the repair in the log; you tested your work, and the whole net leaned on it without ever knowing'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='for ten loud minutes the net is perfect, then the storm's first real gust shakes the grange hall and the crackle comes snarling back mid-report; the connector you did not test has popped loose, and a hail report dissolves into static'>>

choices.label: Scramble to catch what you can.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=92 beats='Marisol calmly swaps to the little backup antenna and the net limps through, but two reports are lost for good and the log has holes; nobody is hurt and the storm rolls on east; taping a fix is half the job, she says, proving it is the other half, and next week the two of you will reseat that connector together'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='you unspool the fresh cable and Marisol boosts you where the rafters are high; hook by hook the new line goes up and over the squashed section like a detour around a broken bridge; the roof drums with the first rain'>>

choices.label: Crimp the connectors and hook it to the radio.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the crimps bite and the new line is in, but it runs a good deal longer than the old one, and Marisol taps the meter: long cable can bleed signal away quietly, she says, and the far spotters are the ones you lose first'>>

choices.label: Look at what the parts bin offers.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='in the parts bin sits the club's little inline amplifier, which would boost the long run but adds one more thing that can fail in a storm; keep it simple and plug straight in, or add the amp and give the far voices a ladder'>>

choices.label: Wire in the inline amplifier.
Keep it simple and plug straight in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the amp hums to life and the meter jumps; Pine Hollow comes in like they are calling from downstairs; as the squall line arrives, a nervous new spotter calls in something he thinks is a funnel cloud, and Marisol slides her chair beside yours'>>

choices.label: Help sort out the scary-looking report.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='with the signal crisp you ask the spotter careful questions off Marisol's card: is it rotating, is it attached to the cloud base; it is not, it is scud, raggedy harmless cloud that loves to frighten people, and the correct all-clear goes out under your callsign'>>

choices.label: See the net through to the storm's end.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=92 beats='the storm crosses the county without one report lost; your detour line and its humming amplifier carried every voice, including the one that needed to hear it was only scud; at closedown Marisol tells the club the attic has never sounded better, and your bypass becomes the permanent line'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='simple is quick, and quick feels fine until the squall line arrives; nearby spotters are clear, but Pine Hollow and the north farms fade to whispers on the long unboosted run, and you lean into the headset until your ears ache, pulling words out of the hiss'>>

choices.label: Copy everything you possibly can.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=92 beats='you save every close-in report, but Pine Hollow's hail measurement never makes it through, and the weather service asks about the gap in the county's log; the storm passes with everyone safe; over cocoa, Marisol shows you the arithmetic of cable loss, and the amplifier goes in first thing at the next meeting'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the logbook is yours; you settle the headset, square the pages, and answer your first check-in with a voice steadier than your hands; behind you Marisol works along the crackling cable with her flashlight, half listening, always close'>>

choices.label: Find your rhythm as the reports come in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the squall line steps onto the county like something with feet: pea hail at Dutchman's Corner, forty mile an hour gusts on Route 9, a barn door gone at the Klein farm; you log it all, time, place, spotter, exactly like the drills'>>

choices.label: Answer the next call on the repeater.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='between check-ins a thin, unfamiliar voice surfaces on the repeater, half swallowed by static: anybody, please, Miller Ridge, the trail is washed out; your stomach drops; Marisol is across the attic with her head in the rafters, still tracing the bad cable'>>

choices.label: Act the way the club trained you to.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='the training card is taped beside the mic: stay calm, get facts, get help; you can answer the caller yourself, slow and steady the way Marisol taught you, or wave her over right now and back her up with the logbook while she takes the mic'>>

choices.label: Answer the caller yourself, calm and slow.
Wave Marisol over and back her up on the log.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you key the mic: station calling about Miller Ridge, I copy you, this is the storm club net; the voice steadies, a teenage hiker named Jordan, a day hike gone long, the trail washed out below, and the sky to the west gone dark green'>>

choices.label: Get the facts, one question at a time.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='one question at a time, straight off the card: Jordan is alone but unhurt, passed the split boulder an hour ago, and can see the old fire tower up the slope; you print each fact in the log in big letters; Marisol is beside you now, hand on your shoulder, letting you keep the mic'>>

choices.label: Work out the fastest way to get Jordan safe.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='two good plans, minutes apart: switch radio two to the sheriff's net and relay Jordan's position to the deputies while Marisol keeps Jordan company on radio one, or use the wall map to guide Jordan up to the fire tower's ground floor shelter room while Marisol phones the ranger station'>>

choices.label: Relay Jordan's position to the sheriff's net.
Guide Jordan to the fire tower shelter by the map.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you build the relay card the way the drills taught: who, where, landmarks, condition, time; then you key radio two and read it to the sheriff's dispatcher without one wasted word; she reads it back perfectly and says units are rolling'>>

choices.label: Hold the net steady while help rolls.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='radio one: Marisol keeps Jordan talking and moving upslope; radio two: the deputy calls out mile markers as the rain arrives; the two stories race each other across your log pages toward the fire tower'>>

choices.label: Log the moment they meet.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the deputy and the ranger reach the tower just as the gust front combs the ridge, and Jordan is inside, wrapped in a blanket, asking if the radio kid is still there; the dispatcher calls your relay textbook; Marisol uses the word every operator wants, professional; the storm howls past a shelter with everyone inside it'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='the wall map becomes a trail under your finger: from the split boulder, up and left of the rockslide, six hundred steps to the tower; you talk Jordan through it landmark by landmark while Marisol reads the ranger the same picture over the phone; wind claws at the attic window like it wants to listen'>>

choices.label: Stay on the mic until Jordan is through the door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='I am inside, Jordan says, and the repeater carries the clunk of the shelter door just as hail starts hammering the tower roof; the ranger arrives once the line has passed and finds one soggy hiker and, still on the radio, one steady young voice keeping company; Marisol logs it simply: caller sheltered, guided by the second seat'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Marisol takes the mic in one smooth motion and becomes pure calm; you become her second: logbook open, pencil flying, repeating back each fact she draws out of the hiker, Jordan, alone, unhurt, above the washout near the split boulder'>>

choices.label: Make yourself exactly as useful as possible.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='Marisol covers the mic and gives you a real operator's choice: plot Jordan's landmarks on the wall map and hand her a bearing the ranger can use, or take radio two and keep the spotter net's storm reports flowing so the county picture does not go dark during the rescue'>>

choices.label: Plot the landmarks and work out a bearing.
Keep the storm net alive on radio two.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='split boulder pin, washout pin, fire tower pin; you lay the ruler between them and read off a bearing and a distance in your steadiest voice; Marisol repeats it to the ranger word for word, and the map under your hands quietly becomes a rescue'>>

choices.label: Watch your bearing do its work.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=92 beats='the ranger walks your bearing almost exactly and radios back found and fine as the squall line sweeps the ridge behind them; after closedown Marisol taps the three pins and tells the whole club that tonight the map did the rescuing; the pins stay where you put them, a tiny monument in the attic'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='radio two is a river and you keep it in its banks: hail size at Bell Creek, wind over the fairgrounds, a power flash near the quarry, logged, logged, logged, while two feet away Marisol talks a stranger up a dark hillside'>>

choices.label: Carry the net through the height of the storm.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='when it is over, Jordan is safe in the fire tower and your pages are the only complete record of the storm's march across the county; reading the log back, you can see the whole squall line like a creature drawn in columns, gust by gust; the weather service asks for a copy; you never said a word on the rescue, and it could not have happened without your pages'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the ops table is a county map under scratched plastic, grease pencils on strings, a laptop looping radar, and the official forecast pinned at the corner: squall line crossing the north hills at four fifteen; Ms. Okafor hands you a grease pencil like it is a key to something'>>

choices.label: Learn the plotter's job.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='every spotter report becomes a mark: an X, a time, a word like hail or gust; Dev, a high schooler with radar on his laptop, calls out fixes from the attic net and you plot them; slowly the storm stops being weather and becomes a shape that moves'>>

choices.label: Notice what the marks are starting to say.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='your last three X's do not sit on the forecast track; the line's south end looks faster and farther south than the pinned map says it should be; Ms. Okafor sees you frowning and offers two jobs: dig into the plots and test the official track, or run the timeline board that keeps the whole room current'>>

choices.label: Re-plot the last hour and test the forecast track.
Run the timeline board for the room.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you re-plot the hour clean on tracing paper: the south stations show pressure falling faster and gusts arriving earlier; the line is not a straight wall, its south end is bulging forward like a runner leaning into a corner'>>

choices.label: Extend the line forward in time.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you walk the bulge forward with the ruler, and your pencil stops on the county fairgrounds, where the harvest festival is in full swing, at about three forty, thirty five minutes earlier than the official track says and miles farther south'>>

choices.label: Take a breath, because this is a big claim.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='Ms. Okafor's first rule of forecasting is to check yourself before you announce yourself; you can test your line against fresh ground truth by sending questions up to the radio room for the two south spotters, or test it against the science by pulling the latest radar frames with Dev and measuring the line's speed directly'>>

choices.label: Get fresh fixes from the south spotters.
Measure the storm's motion on radar with Dev.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='you write two tight questions, what, where, when, and send them up the attic stairs; the answers come back in minutes: gust front already past Route 9 south, hail starting at Bell Creek; you plot the fresh fixes with a thumping heart'>>

choices.label: See where the new marks land.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='both fixes land on your pencil line like they were invited; Dev whistles low; Ms. Okafor studies your tracing paper, then the clock, and says the words you will remember all year: alright, forecaster, present your case'>>

choices.label: Get ready to present.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='how do you argue something this big: walk her through the map itself, mark by mark and time by time, and let the evidence do the talking, or lead with the headline, the festival gets hit at three forty, and then show the work behind it'>>

choices.label: Walk her through the marks and let the map argue.
Lead with the headline, then show the work.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='mark by mark you retell the storm: here at two ten, here at two twenty five, faster with every step, and her pencil follows yours; she re-checks two plots herself, nods once, and picks up the phone to the county emergency manager with your tracing paper in her hand'>>

choices.label: Listen to the call your map started.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='the emergency manager asks hard questions and Ms. Okafor answers them straight off your plots; the festival announcer moves everyone into the livestock barns and the grandstand shelter, grumbling and laughing, cotton candy and all, while the west goes the color of a bruise'>>

choices.label: Watch the clock you predicted.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the gust front hits the empty midway at three forty three, three minutes off your pencil line; funnel cake stands roll and nobody is under them; afterward the emergency manager visits the ops table to see the tracing paper for himself, and Ms. Okafor introduces you as the forecaster who caught the turn; the state storm report notes that a local club flagged the acceleration first'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='the festival gets hit at three forty, you say, and the room goes quiet; Ms. Okafor grills you like a scientist, where is your ground truth, why is the official track wrong, and every question lands on a mark you have already made; it costs some sweaty minutes, but the case holds'>>

choices.label: Stand by while she makes the call.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=92 beats='the warning goes out with a little less lead time than it might have had, but it goes, and the festival crowd is snug in the barns when the wind arrives; afterward Ms. Okafor shares a secret of the trade: a headline opens ears, but the boring careful marks are what open minds; next time, she says, hand them the map first and you will save the argument minutes'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Dev steps the radar back frame by frame and you measure with the screen ruler: the south end of the line is doing forty five miles an hour where the forecast assumed thirty; the math is clean and the bulge is real'>>

choices.label: Check the numbers twice.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the numbers hold, but Dev points at the timestamp: the newest frame is already six minutes old, and Ms. Okafor asks the question that matters, how fresh is this picture, because a warning is only as good as its ground truth'>>

choices.label: Decide what your answer will be.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='you can make the brief airtight by adding the latest spotter plots to the radar measurement, marrying the math to fresh ground truth, or you can call it now on the radar alone, because the clock is running and a festival is a big place to move'>>

choices.label: Add fresh spotter plots before calling it in.
Call it in now on the radar math alone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='the newest spotter fixes drop onto the radar vector like stitches pulling one seam tight; map and math agree on three forty at the fairgrounds; Ms. Okafor reads your one page brief, initials it, and dials the emergency manager without a wasted breath'>>

choices.label: Follow your nowcast out into the world.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='because the brief is airtight, the emergency manager does not stop to argue, and the saved minutes become shelter time; the festival is under roofs early enough that kids watch the shelf cloud roll in from the barn doors like a parade; Ms. Okafor pins your brief to the club wall: one page, two kinds of proof, zero people in the open'>>

ending.title: The Map and the Math Agree

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the call goes out fast, and fast turns out to be fuzzy: the emergency manager asks whether the track is ground-truthed, and the honest answer is not yet; he holds the warning while a deputy drives out to eyeball the sky, and you watch minutes you gave away tick past'>>

choices.label: Help win the lost minutes back.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=95 beats='Ms. Okafor rescues it, feeding fresh spotter fixes to the county while you plot them at speed, and the festival still gets under cover, closer to the wire than anyone liked; nobody is hurt; in the debrief she draws a triangle on the board, radar, spotters, plot, and says a nowcast stands on all three legs or it wobbles; you skipped one leg once, and you will never skip it again'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the timeline board is chalk and columns: time, place, report; you claim it, square your chalk, and start turning radio chatter into a story the whole room can read at a glance; within minutes Ms. Okafor is steering the table by your columns'>>

choices.label: Make the board even easier to read.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='you invent a color code on the fly, green for rain, blue for hail, orange for wind, and the board turns into a weather comic strip marching down the wall; Dev photographs it for the club scrapbook; then your own columns show you something nobody has said out loud'>>

choices.label: Say out loud what the board is showing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='the orange marks are stepping straight toward Cedar Flats, where the middle school soccer semifinal kicks off at three thirty, and the storm will get there first; the room is busy and this is your find; you can build the warning yourself, draft it and get Ms. Okafor's sign-off, or hand the warning job to Dev and keep the board current, because the whole room steers by it'>>

choices.label: Draft the warning and get it signed.
Hand the warning to Dev and keep the board current.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you write it the way Ms. Okafor teaches, what, when, what to do: strong gusts and hail reaching Cedar Flats near three twenty five, clear the field by three fifteen, shelter in the gym, wait for the club's all-clear; she reads it twice and signs the corner'>>

choices.label: Now get it to the right ears.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='the field house phone rings busy, busy, busy, and the clock does not care; two ways to reach the right ears: Dev can dig the athletic director's cell number out of the league sheet, or you can call the snack stand's posted number, which a parent always answers, and have your warning hand-carried to the press box'>>

choices.label: Track down the athletic director's cell with Dev.
Call the snack stand and send the warning by hand.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='the athletic director wavers: the sky over the field looks fine, parents drove a long way, and calling a delay on a bright-ish afternoon takes nerve; you can give him your best evidence in plain words, or offer him a deal that makes the decision easy to live with'>>

choices.label: Give him the evidence, plain and clear.
Offer him the club's deal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you keep it kid-clear: hail the size of peas is falling right now at Bell Creek, which sits upstream of the field on the storm's track, the gust front should arrive near three twenty five, and the sky always looks fine just before a gust front, that is the trap'>>

choices.label: Let the upstream fact land.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='the word upstream does it, and you can hear him deciding; the announcement crackles across the field behind his voice, and the stands begin flowing toward the gym doors while the west stays deceptively bright'>>

choices.label: Stay on the line while the field clears.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the gust front arrives at three thirty four and flattens both corner flags in front of a beautifully empty field; from the gym doorway the teams watch the rain wall cross the pitch like a curtain being drawn; the athletic director keeps you on the line through all of it, and afterward asks whether the club does this every storm; Ms. Okafor writes one line under your warning: sent, heeded, nobody outside'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='here is the club's offer, you say: delay kickoff forty minutes, and we will watch the storm off your field minute by minute and call you the all-clear the moment the line is past; a pause, whistles, crowd noise, and then, deal, kid'>>

choices.label: Keep the club's side of the bargain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the line crosses the empty field at three thirty five while both teams wait dry in the gym; at four eleven your plots show the back edge clear of Cedar Flats, and Ms. Okafor lets you make the all-clear call yourself; the semifinal kicks off at four forty on a soaked, sparkling field, and the league writes the club a thank you letter that goes straight onto the wall'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='a cheerful parent volunteer answers over popcorn noise; you dictate the warning slowly, what is coming, when it arrives, clear the field by three fifteen, shelter in the gym, wait for the club's all-clear, and she reads it back twice and sprints for the press box with the note in her fist'>>

choices.label: Listen for the announcement.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=92 beats='the public address system crackles and a puzzled announcer reads your warning word for word, and because it is written down, nothing gets garbled; the field clears with twelve minutes to spare and both teams watch the gust front from the gym; afterward the note itself, popcorn grease and all, is pinned to the club wall beside the plots, proof that a message with a person attached always finds its way'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Dev takes the warning and runs with it; you stay on the board, and staying turns out to be a real job: reports double as the line closes in, and your columns are the only thing keeping fourteen voices from talking over each other'>>

choices.label: Hold the county picture together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='two spotters disagree: one puts the hail line at Bell Creek, the other at the quarry, and both cannot be true; the board is the room's truth, so what it says matters; you can plot both with question marks and radio for a tiebreaker fix, or pick the likelier report, Bell Creek fits the storm's motion, and keep the board clean and confident'>>

choices.label: Plot both with question marks and ask for a tiebreaker.
Pick Bell Creek and keep the board clean.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='your two question marks sit on the board like raised hands, honest about what the room does not know; three minutes later a third spotter breaks the tie, the quarry report was old hail already melted and Bell Creek is live, and you erase exactly one question mark'>>

choices.label: Notice what the question marks just did.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='after the storm, Ms. Okafor circles your two chalk question marks and tells the club they were the smartest thing on the board all night: doubt shown exactly where doubt lived, so nobody steered on a guess; the club votes it a standing rule, uncertain reports get question marks and a tiebreaker; the board never lied to the room, not even for three minutes, and that, she says, is the whole game'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='clean and confident, and quietly wrong: the Bell Creek report was the stale one, and for ten minutes the room nudges spotters and warnings a mile off the storm's true hail line, until a puzzled voice from the quarry reports stones bouncing off his truck right now'>>

choices.label: Fix the board fast.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=95 beats='you and Ms. Okafor reset the column together in front of everyone, and the room recovers quickly because everything else on your board is good; no harm reaches anyone, but the ten crooked minutes sting; in the debrief she hands you the chalk and asks what you would do differently, and you draw a question mark; exactly, she says, a board is allowed to be unsure, it is not allowed to be wrong on purpose'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='the club van smells of coffee and electronics; Ray, big laugh, laminated checklist, drives; Priya, quick and precise, runs instruments; before the engine turns over she gives the speech: we watch storms from the side that cannot reach us, we leave early, we never drive through water, and inside this van, weather is homework, not a monster'>>

choices.label: Roll out with the field team.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you inventory the kit hand over hand: anemometer mast, spare rain gauge bottles, hail pads in their frames, the camera, the ruler card, and the weather radio already murmuring with the attic post's net; Priya declares you instruments crew, official'>>

choices.label: Check the route sheet with Ray.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='two jobs on the route sheet and time for one before the line arrives, maybe a piece of the second: the gauge run, swapping rain gauge bottles at three farms along Creek Road so the storm's rainfall gets measured, or setting up the observation post on Windmill Hill, the safe flank overlook, with the mast, the hail pads, and the camera'>>

choices.label: Start with the gauge run along Creek Road.
Set up the Windmill Hill post before the sky closes in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='stops one and two are clockwork: unscrew, cap, label, swap, thirty seconds a bottle, while Ray keeps the van pointed out the gate, nose toward the road, leave-early rule made of steel; westward the sky is going green-gray and the wind sock at the Hutch place stands straight out'>>

choices.label: Head for the third farm.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the Andersen farm is chaos with a fence around it: goats loose in the yard, ducks everywhere, a hay tarp cracking like a flag, and Mrs. Andersen alone in the middle of it because her helper is stuck in town; the rain gauge sits calmly by the barn, doing its one job'>>

choices.label: Pile out and size it up.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='the radio says twenty five minutes until the gust front, and Ray is already parking nose-out; you can help the farm first, animals and tarp, and grab the gauge bottle if time allows, or do protocol first, swap the gauge in ninety seconds while your hands remember how, then throw everything you have at the yard'>>

choices.label: Help with the animals and the tarp first.
Swap the gauge fast, then help the farm.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Priya teaches you the feed bucket trick, rattle it and walk, never chase, and the goats decide you are the most interesting thing on the farm; one by one they follow you into the barn, and you latch the pens while the ducks file in under the workbench like commuters'>>

choices.label: Now the flapping tarp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the tarp fights like a sail, but Ray anchors the corners with old tires while you and Priya haul the lines; the first fat raindrops smack the dust around your shoes, and the radio on Ray's belt says the gust front is ten minutes out'>>

choices.label: Help Mrs. Andersen count the goats.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='eleven goats in the pen and Mrs. Andersen counts twelve on her fingers: Butterscotch is missing, probably sulking down in the creek willows; ten minutes is enough for a fast two minute look with a hard stop, or it is enough to get every person inside right now and trust that goats are better at weather than people are'>>

choices.label: Take a fast two-minute look at the willows with Priya.
Get everyone inside now, people before goats.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Priya sets a timer out loud, two minutes and we walk, and you jog to the willows under a sky like a held breath; there, a cream colored rump backed into the blackberry: Butterscotch, tangled, indignant, and very loud about it; you work the brambles while Priya watches the west and the timer both'>>

choices.label: Free her and run for the barn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='the bucket rattle does the rest; Butterscotch trots, you run, and Ray's horn is honking everyone in as the wind arrives like a shoulder against the whole farm; barn latched, people in the farmhouse, and the gust front breaks over a buttoned-up yard'>>

choices.label: Watch the storm do its worst to a ready farm.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='hail rattles the farmhouse windows like popcorn while twelve goats chew calmly in a latched barn; when the sun knifes back under the cloud line, the yard is soaked, the tarp held, and the gauge bottle rides labeled in the van; Mrs. Andersen will not stop telling the story of the storm club kid and the blackberry goat; Priya logs it: all animals secured, all people sheltered, margin respected, and the timer never had to say so'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='people first, you say, and nobody argues; Mrs. Andersen, Priya, Ray, and you are inside the sturdy farmhouse with the door braced when the gust front arrives, and it arrives like a train made of wind; through the window the willows go over sideways and spring back up again'>>

choices.label: Look for Butterscotch once the line has passed.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the storm passes, loud but brief, and Butterscotch strolls out of the willows soggy, cross, and completely fine, because critters know how to hunker; Mrs. Andersen says you chose exactly right and feeds everyone cider; Ray writes the club's oldest rule in the log where you can see it, animals survive weather better than people survive risks; the gauge bottle gets swapped in the after-storm calm, data safe, everyone dry'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='ninety seconds, just like the drills: unscrew, cap, label with the farm name and the time, fresh bottle in, and the storm's rainfall record for Creek Road is safe in the crate; then it is all hands to the yard, tarp lines, feed bucket, ducks scattering like spilled popcorn'>>

choices.label: Throw everything at the yard.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='goats penned, ducks under the bench, and the radio says the gust front is eight minutes out and already visible from Windmill Hill; Ray backs the van to the farmhouse and stands by the horn; the hay tarp is half anchored, two tires on, two corners still snapping'>>

choices.label: Make the call on the tarp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='Ray gives you the honest arithmetic: one more minute finishes the tarp, and one more minute is exactly the margin the club promises never to spend; leave the tarp and get inside now, hay gets wet and people do not, or take the minute and throw the last two tires on with Ray, cutting it close'>>

choices.label: Leave the tarp, everyone inside now.
Take one more minute and finish it with Ray.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='from the farmhouse window you watch the wind take the tarp like a magician's tablecloth trick; it wraps the fence and stays there, flapping furiously, while the hay underneath goes gray with rain; inside, the kettle is on and every heartbeat in the room is slow'>>

choices.label: Tally what was lost and what was not.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the tally after the storm: one soaked haystack, one tarp rescued off the fence, zero scratches on anybody, and one perfectly labeled gauge bottle that tells the county exactly how hard Creek Road was rained on; Mrs. Andersen shrugs about the hay, it will dry, hay does; Priya writes your first field report with you, and its last line is the club's whole creed: we can always buy hay'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the last two tires go on and the tarp is drum tight, and then the minute is gone and the wind arrives while you and Ray are still crossing the yard; a bucket cartwheels past, grit stings your ears, the first gust shoves you like a crowd, and Ray's hand clamps your arm for the last five steps through the farmhouse door'>>

choices.label: Catch your breath inside.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=95 beats='the tarp holds and nobody is hurt, and Ray is quiet in a way that is worse than shouting; that was my mistake as much as yours, he says at last, we do not spend our margin on hay; the ride home is silent, and at the clubhouse the two of you write it up together for the safety book, new rule underlined: when the horn is worth honking, the job is already over; your gauge bottle still makes it into the data crate, but the lesson rides home heavier'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Windmill Hill earns its name: wind combing the grass flat and the whole western sky one long shelf cloud, like a rolling pin of slate; Ray parks nose-out on the east track, escape route agreed before anyone opens a door, and Priya starts handing you the mast sections'>>

choices.label: Build the observation post.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='mast up and guyed, hail pads staked in their frames, camera on the tripod with the windmill for scale; the anemometer wakes, fifteen miles an hour gusting twenty two; Priya points at the shelf cloud and teaches you the difference that matters, outflow shelf, not a rotating wall, awe, not alarm; the attic net wants a wind reading every five minutes'>>

choices.label: Split the post's two jobs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='two jobs, one you: instruments captain, owning the readings and calling them to the attic net on schedule, or camera captain, documenting the shelf cloud's arrival and the hail for the club's science archive, every shot timestamped; Priya will back up whichever one you do not take'>>

choices.label: Take the instruments and call the readings.
Take the camera and document the storm.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you find the rhythm fast: Windmill Hill, two eight gusting four one, and the attic post reads it back and relays it onward; between calls Priya shows you how the gusts come in families, build, peak, breathe; the shelf cloud swallows the last blue in the west'>>

choices.label: Keep calling as the numbers climb.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='fifty one miles an hour and the first hail ticks off the van roof like thrown gravel; Ray calls everyone inside the van, windows up, and announces the load-and-leave threshold: when he says go, the post comes down and the van rolls, no debate; Priya raises the real question, the mast'>>

choices.label: Settle the mast question.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='strike the mast now and finish the run on the handheld anemometer through the cracked window, gear safe and numbers humbler, or leave the mast up until Ray's deadline and read the remote display from inside the van, chasing the peak gust right up to the moment the post comes down'>>

choices.label: Leave the mast up till Ray's deadline and log the peak.
Strike the mast now and switch to the handheld.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=104 beats='from inside the van you call the numbers off the remote display while hail pops on the roof: five two, five five, then the gust front's leading edge itself, five eight, and the whole van rocks on its springs like a boat; Ray says go, the mast comes down slick as a drill, and the van is rolling east inside his margin'>>

choices.label: Ride home with the number.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='back at the grange hall the attic log confirms it: Windmill Hill, fifty eight miles an hour, the highest wind measured anywhere in the county today, logged at the exact minute the gust front crossed the ridge, with your callsign on the reading'>>

choices.label: Hand the data over.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the weather service uses the club's Windmill Hill reading in the official storm report, and fifty eight becomes the number everyone quotes at school on Monday; the mast has a new dent and the hail pads look like the moon, which Priya calls the best kind of trophy; Ray taps the safety log too, post struck on deadline, van rolling on time, and that pairing, the great number and the kept margin, is what the club frames for the wall'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the mast comes down dry and safe in its case, and you finish the run on the handheld through the cracked window: four four is your best, solid data honestly gotten, while the real peak howls by unmeasured somewhere over the grass; out in the weather, the hail pads are still taking notes'>>

choices.label: Collect the hail pads after the all-clear.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='the pads come in stippled with perfect little craters, a record of the hail even though the peak gust got away; Priya spreads them on the workbench and shows you the trade you made without knowing it, gear kept safe versus the biggest number lost, and every field scientist makes that trade over and over; next storm, she says, you will know the price of both answers before you choose, and that is exactly what a second storm is for'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the camera loves the shelf cloud: you frame it behind the windmill for scale, timestamp burning in the corner, while Priya narrates the structure like a tour guide, outflow lifting the dust line, rain shaft dragging behind like a broom; you shoot the gust front racing across the valley floor toward the hill'>>

choices.label: Keep shooting as the hail arrives.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='pea hail, then marbles, and Ray calls everyone into the van to shoot through glass; when the line passes and Ray gives the all-clear, you will have only minutes before the evidence melts: measure and photograph the three biggest stones on the hail pad with the ruler card, the archive's first calibrated hail record, or chase the light east, because the back edge is lifting and there will be a double rainbow over the wet valley'>>

choices.label: Measure the hail on the pad before it melts.
Chase the light for the club's best-ever photo.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='all-clear, and you are out the door with the ruler card: biggest stone nineteen millimeters, photographed square on the pad grid with the timestamp, then the next two, while meltwater eats the small ones around your knees; Priya calls the technique textbook'>>

choices.label: File the archive's first calibrated hail record.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='nineteen millimeters at Windmill Hill becomes the county's official hail measurement for the storm, photographed, calibrated, and filed in the club's science archive with your name on the entry; the weather service cites it, and Priya opens the winter class on hail with your photo on the projector; the rainbow happened too, and someone else got a blurry picture of it, and you do not mind even slightly'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='you chase the light east and the sky pays out: a double rainbow braced over the soaked valley, mammatus pouches glowing under the storm's retreating anvil, and you shoot the best photograph in the club's whole scrapbook while the hail pads sit forgotten in the grass, going drip, drip, drip'>>

choices.label: Bring your treasure back to the van.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=95 beats='the photo wins the fall fair and hangs framed in the grange hall, and every time you see it you also see what is missing: the archive entry that reads hail, observed, unmeasured, because the stones melted while you chased the light; beautiful, Priya says, meaning it, and next time we will get the data too; the club gains its greatest picture, and you gain the quiet lesson that in field science, pretty is optional and measured is forever'>>

ending.title: The Best Photo, the Lost Data
